pattern: After i operations, the state is fully described by how many were taken from the front, so a 1-D DP over that count captures every reachable configuration.
hints: Each operation removes one element from either end; after i operations exactly i elements are gone.
If you have taken some number of elements from the front, the rest came from the back, which pins down both boundaries.
Let dp[left] be the best score after the current step having taken 'left' from the front; update it from the previous step choosing the front or the back element.

edge_inputs.description: front vs back choice matters with negatives
m equals n, every element used

stress.description: moderate n with up to 300 multipliers
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randrange(1, max(2, min(size, 300)) + 1)
    n = rng.randrange(m, m + 200)
    nums = [rng.randrange(-1000, 1001) for _ in range(n)]
    mult = [rng.randrange(-1000, 1001) for _ in range(m)]
    return [nums, mult]
